pattern: A palindrome is fully decided by its mirrored pairs, so only mismatched pairs need a change, and the smallest valid change is always the earlier-alphabet letter of the two.
hints: A string is a palindrome exactly when position i and position (n-1-i) match for every i, so scan inward from both ends at once.
Wherever a pair already matches, leave it alone -- any change there would only cost an extra operation for nothing.

Wherever a pair disagrees, set both positions to whichever of the two original letters is earlier in the alphabet; that fixes the pair in one operation and can never be beaten for lexicographic order.

edge_inputs.description: odd length, all distinct, middle untouched

stress.description: random lowercase strings up to the length cap
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz') for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
